scope_boundary: Planning runtime-replan delta audit only. The audit validates two deterministic synthetic runtime-replan traces and rejects nine expected-invalid controls for authority widening, stop-condition erasure, unaffected-node rerun without dependency impact, missing residual ownership, missing context delta, missing verification delta, blocked-authority dispatch receipt, support-promotion overclaim, and missing non-claim boundary. It does not execute a deployed planner, runtime scheduler, live feedback loop, decomposition-quality evaluator, context-demand predictor, route-quality evaluator, selected-tier adequacy evaluator, scheduler-optimality test, benchmark, model-quality evaluation, safety evaluation, or deployed replanning system.
transition_reason: The Planning runtime-replan delta audit is useful because it makes authority preservation, stop-condition preservation, affected-subgraph scope, context deltas, verification deltas, residual ownership, blocked-authority no-dispatch behavior, non-claim boundaries, and support-state non-promotion executable as finite Planning records. It remains a deterministic synthetic record gate, not deployed planner evidence, so this accepted decision blocks promotion for deployed planner behavior, runtime scheduler behavior, decomposition quality, context-demand prediction, selected-tier adequacy, route quality, scheduler optimality, live feedback handling, deployed runtime replanning, benchmark performance, model quality, safety, ASI, or the Planning chapter core claim.
required_artifacts: deployed or externally reviewable runtime-replanning trace
planner harness with real or externally replayed plan deltas
runtime scheduler trace with dispatch receipts and blocked-node evidence
decomposition-quality measurement over task objectives and constraints
scheduler-optimality or cost-quality comparison with visible residuals
accepted evidence-transition record for any narrowed upward support movement
negative_results: unaffected-node rerun without dependency impact is rejected
support-promotion overclaim from fixture shape is rejected
no chapter core claim is promoted
downgrade_triggers: authority widening, stop-condition erasure, unaffected rerun, missing residual ownership, missing context delta, missing verification delta, blocked-authority dispatch, support-promotion overclaim, or missing non-claim control accepted as valid
audit described as deployed planner, runtime-scheduler, decomposition-quality, route-quality, selected-tier-adequacy, live-feedback, benchmark, model-quality, safety, ASI, or support-state evidence
support-state promotion without a separate accepted upward transition
promotion_burden: Move above argument only for a narrowed Planning runtime-replan claim after deployed or externally reviewable runtime-replanning traces, planner-harness evidence, scheduler dispatch and blocked-node traces, decomposition-quality measurement, context-demand prediction evidence, selected-tier or route-quality evaluation, scheduler-optimality or cost-quality comparison with visible residuals, live feedback handling traces, residual accounting, and independent review are recorded with a separate accepted upward transition.
limitations: deterministic synthetic runtime-replan delta fixture only
does not promote any chapter core claim
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; not an independent planner, scheduler, decomposition-quality, route-quality, selected-tier, live-feedback, benchmark, source-interpretation, or external safety audit
acceptance_blockers: no deployed or externally reviewable runtime-replanning trace
no planner harness with real or externally replayed plan deltas
no route-quality or selected-tier adequacy evaluation
non_claims: does not promote Planning runtime-replan evidence above argument

does not prove deployed planner behavior, runtime scheduler behavior, decomposition quality, context-demand prediction, route quality, selected-tier adequacy, scheduler optimality, live feedback handling, deployed runtime replanning, benchmark performance, model quality, safety, or ASI
does not create an upward support-state transition
does not promote any chapter core claim
does not promote the Planning as a Control Layer chapter core claim
